scientific_integrity.detail: The evaluated outputs remained procedural and did not invent scientific findings beyond the provided study framing.

practice_boundaries.detail: The package stays in protocol or application drafting scope and does not provide patient-care advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflows preserve review-design or funding-application conventions instead of improvising unsupported methodology.

code_usability.detail: The primary deliverables are structured documents rather than scientific analysis code.

functional_suitability.note: The package decomposes the protocol into administrative, introduction, and methods sections effectively.

reliability.note: The helper script works, and the section constraints reduce drift.

performance_context.note: The single-script helper and section-level prompt decomposition keep execution cost proportional to PICOS complexity.

agent_usability.note: The staged title/PICOS validation improves usability for review authors.

human_usability.note: Title-validation rules and PICOS prompting give users clear entry criteria and prevent wasted protocol generation runs.

security.note: The workflow remains bounded to protocol drafting and avoids medical treatment claims.

maintainability.note: The package is compact and easy to reason about.

agent_specific.note: The rules are strong, though concrete output examples for invalid titles would improve edge handling.

assertions.note: The output shape remains checklist-driven and audit-friendly.

assertions.text: The output preserves registration-specific formatting constraints and placeholder handling.
assertions.note: Registry field behavior is kept explicit.

assertions.text: Missing title or PICO anchors trigger a constrained recovery path instead of fabricated registry content.
assertions.note: The request contained adequate registry anchors.

key_strengths: The title-validation gate keeps review protocols from starting on a weak foundation.
Section-level decomposition makes the protocol easier to generate and audit.
Current-date handling is explicit, which helps keep search windows reproducible.

recommendations.title: Add one invalid-title remediation example
recommendations.problem: Users know that titles are validated, but they do not see a canonical recovery pattern.
recommendations.root_cause: The package documents the rule set more strongly than the recovery behavior.
recommendations.fix: Add a short example showing how to rewrite a title that misses PICOS or exceeds the length budget.
